content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 18,
1992
Frances Hong
City Clerk
City of Stockton
425 North El Dorado Street
Stockton,
CA
95202—1997
Re:
Your Request For Advice
Our File No.
1-92-569
Dear Ms.
Hong:
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
!
Because your request
is a general inquiry,
we treat it as one for informal assistance
rsuant to Regulation 18329(c).
2
?
QUESTIONS
What are the filing requirements for a single proponent
expending personal funds for the qualification of a ballot
measure?
CONCLUSION
A proponent who makes expenditures from personal funds •for
the qualification of a measure will qualify as a committee when
he/she spends $1,000 or more during a calendar year and will be
required to file independent expenditure committee campaign
statements.
ANALYSIS
Section 82013(b) defines a “committee” as any person or
combination of persons who directly or indirectly makes
independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar
year.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)
T
.:.,. Qr-
—
1)
El..... Qfl7
—
—..-..-.-.‘-, (“.
•
(L) 6


Frances Hong
Page Two
I—92—569
An “independent expenditure” is defined as:
an expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or
defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification,
passage or defeat of a clearly identified
measure,
or taken as a whole and in context,
unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election but which is not
made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee.
Section 82031.
(Emphasis added.)
Furthermore, Regulation 18225(S)
provides that the term
“expenditure,” as used in the Act,
includes any monetary or
noninonetary payment made for the purpose of influencing the action
of the voters for or against the qualification or passage of any
3SUre.
Section 84200(b)
requires an individual who qualifies as an
independent expenditure committee to file campaign statements
(Form 461)
by July 31 for the period ending June 30,
and January
31 for the period ending December 31 if he/she has made
independent expenditures during the six-month period before the
closing date of the statements.
In addition,
a Supplemental
Independent Expenditure Report
(Form 465)
is required to be filed
for any reporting period in which independent expenditures
totaling $500 or more are made to support or oppose a single
candidate or ballot measure,
or the qualification of a ballot
measure.
For further information regarding the filing requirements for
independent expenditure committees, please refer to Campaign
Information Manual E.
If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me Monday through Thursday at
(916)
322-5662.
Sincerely,
Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant

QUESTION: What are the filing requirements for a single proponent
expending personal funds for the qualification of a ballot
measure?

CONCLUSION: A proponent who makes expenditures from personal funds •for
the qualification of a measure will qualify as a committee when
he/she spends $1,000 or more during a calendar year and will be
required to file independent expenditure committee campaign
statements.

Because your request is a general inquiry, we treat it as one for informal assistance rsuant to Regulation 18329(c). 2 ? QUESTIONS What are the filing requirements for a single proponent expending personal funds for the qualification of a ballot measure? CONCLUSION A proponent who makes expenditures from personal funds •for the qualification of a measure will qualify as a committee when he/she spends $1,000 or more during a calendar year and will be required to file independent expenditure committee campaign statements. ANALYSIS Section 82013(b) defines a “committee” as any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly makes independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. Section 82031. (Emphasis added.) Furthermore, Regulation 18225(S) provides that the term “expenditure,” as used in the Act, includes any monetary or noninonetary payment made for the purpose of influencing the action of the voters for or against the qualification or passage of any 3SUre. Section 84200(b) requires an individual who qualifies as an independent expenditure committee to file campaign statements (Form 461) by July 31 for the period ending June 30, and January 31 for the period ending December 31 if he/she has made independent expenditures during the six-month period before the closing date of the statements.
